rulings.Case Title: DB REAL ESTATE PACIFIC PLAZA PARTNERS, L.P. VS. THE ONE SEAFOOD BUFFET, INCORPORATED, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Amended Notice And/Or Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 20, 2024, line 1. DEFENDANTS JUN ZHENG, SAMANTHA HENNINGSUI, LI HE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And/Or Summary Adjudication Of Issues. Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment/Adjudication is OFF CALENDAR. Notice of withdrawal of motion on file. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Determine & Adopt Appraised Value Pursuant To The Partition Of Real Property Act; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Olivia Dajee
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 20, 2024, line 3. PLAINTIFF FREDA LEEPER, AS TRUSTEE'S Motion To Determine & Adopt Appraised Value Pursuant To The Partition Of Real Property Act. Plaintiff's Motion to Determine Value is continued to January 31, 2025. The Court appoints Paula Saling of Bridge-to-Bridge Appraisers to appraise the property. No later than January 21, 2025 a copy of the appraisal to be filed with the Court. rulings.Case Title: LEE GREENBERG VS. JERRY BENJAMIN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 20, 2024, line 4. DEFENDANTS MICHELLE HORNEFF-COHEN, HORNEFF WADDLE CORPORATION'S DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT. Defendant's Demurrer to Amended Complaint is sustained with final leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts in support of each element of each cause of action against the moving defendants. Incomplete and conclusory allegations are not sufficient. The Corut notes that the moving party AGAIN noticed their motions in the wrong department. The Corut further notes that Plaintiff again filed their opposition late. In the future if parties continue to ignore the local rules, rules of court and code of civil procedure, the motions will be ordered off calendar or treated as unopposed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
